zh.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor)於大約1520年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾。她和Sir William Tayler至少有1個女兒。她去世於1639年在她的家鄉享年120歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor)于大约1520年出生在英国英格兰維爾爾。她和Sir William Tayler至少有1个女儿。她去世于1639年在她的家乡享年120岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bothe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Detlef, Manfred, Wolfgang.
North German and Dutch: from the North German personal name Bodo (see Bode 2).

variants.name: Bode
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto, Gerd, Gerhard, Helmut, Armin, Dieter, Erna, Ewald, Friedhelm, Fritz, Gunter.
Dutch and German: occupational name for a messenger or representative, Dutch bode, Middle Low German bode. A bode was a medieval official with a variety of different functions.
German and Danish: from the North German personal name Bodo, a derivative of Old Saxon bodo ‘messenger, leader’.

variants.name: Bottemiller
meanings: Americanized form of a German surname, probably North German Bottemöller, of which the second element (Middle Low German möller) means ‘miller’ and the first one is perhaps a variant of the personal name Bothe . The surname Bottemöller is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Both
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Johann, Otto, Ulrich, Wilhelm.
North German and Dutch: variant of   Bothe  .

Hungarian: variant of Bot, from the old personal name Bot or from the vocabulary word bot ‘stick’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name for a shepherd or a village guard. This surname is also found in Slovakia.

nameMeanings.name: (Taylor)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: occupational name for a tailor, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English taillour ‘tailor’ (Old French tailleor, tailleur; Late Latin taliator, from taliare ‘to cut’). The surname is extremely common in Britain and Ireland. In North America, it has absorbed equivalents from other languages, many of which are also common among Ashkenazic Jews, for example German Schneider and Hungarian Szabo . It is also very common among African Americans.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Terrien   ‘owner of a farmland’ or of its altered forms, such as   Therrien   and   Terrian  .

variants.name: Tayloe
meanings: Altered form of English, Scottish, and Irish   Taylor  .

variants.name: Schneider
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Franz, Erwin, Fritz, Dieter, Manfred, Heinz, Alois, Ewald, Helmut.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, literally ‘cutter’, from Middle High German snīder, German Schneider, Yiddish shnayder. The same term was sometimes used to denote a woodcutter. This surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Hungary, Poland, and Czechia. In Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is also established in Slavicized forms (see 2 below). In part, Schneider is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ). Compare Sneider 1, Snider 1, and Snyder 1.
Germanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Šnajdr or Šnajder, and Czech Šneidr, Šneider, Šnejdr, and Šnejder, surnames of German origin (see above).

variants.name: Tayler
meanings: English: variant of   Taylor  .

variants.name: Tailor
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Pravin, Dilip, Mahesh, Sanjay, Kanchan, Kaushik, Manilal, Mohanlal, Navnit, Shailesh, Ajay, Alpana.
Indian (Gujarat and Mumbai): mainly Parsi occupational name from the English word tailor.
English: variant of   Taylor  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) was born about 1520, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter with Sir William Tayler. She died in 1639, in her hometown, at the age of 120.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) est née environ 1520, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Sir William Tayler. Elle est décédée en 1639, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 120 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) wurde um 1520 in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir William Tayler mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1639 im Alter von 120 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) è nata il circa 1520 nel Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Sir William Tayler. Morì nel 1639, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 120 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor)(은)는 대략 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir William Tayler(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1639년 그녀의 고향에서 120세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) nasceu aproximadamente 1520, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Sir William Tayler. Ela faleceu em 1639, em sua cidade natal, com 120 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) родилась около 1520 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Sir William Tayler. Она умерла в 1639 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 120 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Frideswid Bothe (Taylor) nació aproximadamente 1520, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Sir William Tayler. Murió en 1639, en su pueblo, a la edad de 120 años.
